signals.title: NZD/CAD — G8 Bilateral Theme + Jobs Data
signals.text: NZD/CAD at 0.4370 (implied) — NZD leads G8 performance while CAD lags significantly ahead of today's Canadian jobs data. The 1.297% spread indicates a strong bilateral theme; NZD is supported by a hawkish RBNZ bias (hike priced for 27 May) while CAD faces headwinds from recovering oil prices and soft employment expectations. NZD/CAD upside remains the dominant G8 trend until the BoC meeting on 10 Jun.

signals.title: AUD/USD — Positioning + Retail Divergence
signals.text: AUD/USD at 0.7236 — COT LF net long +48,299 (74.8% long) vs retail 87% short. This extreme institutional vs retail divergence suggests a high-conviction trend; retail is aggressively fading the move while specs remain heavily committed. The -60bp AUD carry premium vs USD supports the bid. Watch for retail capitulation to drive a squeeze toward 0.7300; invalidation on a break below 0.7150.

signals.title: USD/CHF — Geopolitical + Technical
signals.text: USD/CHF at 0.7781 — Price testing two-month lows at 0.7765 after rejection at the 0.7800 handle. Despite a massive +375bp Fed-SNB carry advantage, escalation risks in the Middle East (Strait of Hormuz) sustain CHF safe-haven demand. Carry dominates only if VIX remains below 25; current VIX 17.1 (normal volatility) suggests the technical rejection is driven by geopolitical hedging rather than a full carry unwind.

signals.title: EUR/USD — Correlation Break + NFP
signals.text: EUR/USD vs EuroStoxx correlation at +0.721 vs norm of -0.125 (z=+1.78σ) — the historical inverse relationship has broken as both assets move in tandem on USD weakness. EUR/USD at 1.1770 remains bid ahead of US Nonfarm Payrolls (62K expected). A significant NFP beat restores the USD bid and likely snaps this correlation back toward the mean. Watch 1.1800 resistance.
